title: [InstCombine] Fold `(x < y) ? -1 : zext(x > y)` and `(x > y) ? 1 : sext(x < y)` to `ucmp/scmp(x, y)`

tests.source_program:
define i8 @ucmp_ult_ugt_unsigned_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %gt_bool = icmp ugt i32 %x, %y
  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i8
  %lt = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %lt, i8 -1, i8 %gt
  ret i8 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary explicitly shows signed comparisons (slt, sgt, sext) but does not mention unsigned variants (ult, ugt) with zext. The patch handles both signed and unsigned based on the outer predicate's signedness, but the summary text focuses on signed examples.

alive2.src:
define i8 @ucmp_ult_ugt_unsigned_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %gt_bool = icmp ugt i32 %x, %y
  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i8
  %lt = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %lt, i8 -1, i8 %gt
  ret i8 %r
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i8 @ucmp_ult_ugt_unsigned_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %gt_bool = icmp ugt i32 %x, %y
  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i8
  %lt = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %lt, i8 255, i8 %gt
  ret i8 %r
}
=>
define i8 @ucmp_ult_ugt_unsigned_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %r = ucmp i8 i32 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%r = !range i8 %r, i8 255, i8 2
  ret i8 %#range_0_%r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
define i16 @scmp_i16_return_width_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %gt_bool = icmp sgt i32 %x, %y
  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i16
  %lt = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %lt, i16 -1, i16 %gt
  ret i16 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary shows i8 return type in all examples, but the patch supports any integer width for the zext/sext result. This tests i16 return width which is not explicitly covered in the summary text.

alive2.src:
define i16 @scmp_i16_return_width_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %gt_bool = icmp sgt i32 %x, %y
  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i16
  %lt = icmp slt i32 %x, %y
  %r = select i1 %lt, i16 -1, i16 %gt
  ret i16 %r
}

  %lt = icmp slt i32 %x, %y

tests.source_program:
define i8 @scmp_reversed_operand_order(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %gt_bool = icmp sgt i32 %y, %x
  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i8
  %lt = icmp slt i32 %y, %x
  %r = select i1 %lt, i8 -1, i8 %gt
  ret i8 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary shows pattern as (x < y) and (x > y) with x as first operand. This test uses reversed operand order (y < x, y > x) which is semantically equivalent but syntactically different from the documented patterns.

alive2.src:
define i8 @scmp_reversed_operand_order(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %gt_bool = icmp sgt i32 %y, %x
  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i8
  %lt = icmp slt i32 %y, %x
  %r = select i1 %lt, i8 -1, i8 %gt
  ret i8 %r
}
alive2.log:

  %gt = zext i1 %gt_bool to i8
  %lt = icmp slt i32 %y, %x
  %r = select i1 %lt, i8 255, i8 %gt
  ret i8 %r
}
=>
define i8 @scmp_reversed_operand_order(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %r = scmp i8 i32 %y, %x
  %#range_0_%r = !range i8 %r, i8 255, i8 2
  ret i8 %#range_0_%r
}
